studyInNepalOverview: Nepal is a practical study destination for international students who want a mix of affordability, cultural depth, and academically focused institutions.
visaInfo: International students generally need an approved student visa or permit before studying in Nepal.
Visa requirements can vary by nationality, institution, and level of study.
Always verify the latest requirements with your institution and the relevant government office.
costInfo: Tuition in Nepal is often lower than in many international destinations, but the exact fee depends on the institution and program.
Living cost is usually more manageable in Nepal than in many major Asian study hubs.
Budget for housing, transport, food, and document processing in addition to tuition.
accommodationInfo: Students typically choose between hostels, apartments, or shared rooms near campus.
Kathmandu and Pokhara have the widest range of student accommodation options.
Check safety, transport access, and total monthly cost before deciding.
scholarshipInfo: Some institutions offer merit-based discounts or limited scholarships.
Scholarship availability is competitive and varies by program.
Ask about awards early in the application process.
contactInfo: Use the contact page for detailed admission support.
Use the booking page to request a consultation and planning call.
The WhatsApp button connects you during Nepal business hours.
